title: Baroque Works Fans the Flames
description: On pages 11–14, this part focuses on baroque works fans the flames, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: Baroque Works Fans the Flames. The rebellion and royal army rush toward Alubarna as Vivi’s group searches for a way to stop the war before it starts. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: The next time we meet, consider your life forfeit,

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: Over there! It's the straw-hat pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: Come on, let's go before the marines get here! Which way's Alubarna!?

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: This way! We have to head East!

dialogue.speaker: Royal Army soldier
dialogue.text: Hey Luffy! What're you doing? We've gotta get going!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: I won't let you escape next time!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: I don't think I hate you anymore, Smokie!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Captain! Why aren't you chasing after them!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Call back the marines chasing after them since it's pointless...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And tell them to get all Marine ships in the vicinity of Alabama to gather here in this country.

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: You want to call for reinforce- wants!?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: B-but I'm not sure if the higher-ups will agree to send so many ships just to catch a few pirates...

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: Since when the hell did I care for the opinions of higher-ups?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: I'll go contact them right away!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I can smell Nami's perfume growing stronger!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey guys! We're not gonna run all the way to Alubarma now, are we?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Isn't there like a stable in this town!? Let's borrow some horses!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: But the marimes are swarming all over...

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: Don't worry, guys... Take a look in front of you!

summary: This part develops baroque works fans the flames and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
